notes: v1.1 — Added signal_universe: read-only signal assets the Generator can use in conditions but never hold. Motivated by research phase finding that high-Sharpe symphonies (Sharpe 2.0+) use bond and volatility behavior as leading indicators for equity crashes — not as holdings. The generator without bond signals is deaf to early warning and will always exit too late.

generator_approved.description: Tickers the Generator is allowed to HOLD (allocate capital to). Expand only after Research Phase confirms utility.

expansion_candidates.rationale: Add after Research Phase confirms utility in high-Sharpe symphonies
expansion_candidates.notes: TLT — Research Phase found it used as signal in 6/17 mini-strategies across FNGG+Sisyphus. Backtest as holdable safe-haven alternative to BIL before adding. GLD — appears in Sisyphus as alternative asset. KMLM/BTAL/FTLS — managed futures, no in-universe equivalent found.

signal_universe.description: Tickers the Generator can USE IN CONDITIONS (lhs-val, rhs-val comparators) but NEVER allocate capital to. These are read-only market indicators. This is the fix for the bond-signal gap identified in Research Phase: high-Sharpe symphonies use bond/volatility behavior as LEADING INDICATORS for equity crashes, not as holdings. Without these signals the generator exits positions too late.
signal_universe.usage_rule: Signal assets appear only as lhs-val or rhs-val in if-child condition nodes. They must NEVER appear as asset leaf nodes (step: asset). The Generator must check this rule before outputting any strategy.

bonds.what_they_signal: Treasury/bond market stress. When bond RSI drops or bond price falls below MA, credit stress is incoming — exit leveraged longs BEFORE equity drawdown shows up in SPY price. IEF and TLT often lead equities by 1-5 days.
confirmed_uses: IEF RSI(10) vs PSQ threshold — bond strength vs inverse equity as regime gate (FNGG ms-002, Sisyphus ms-002, ms-006)
TLT 15-day MA return vs UDN threshold — bond trend vs dollar as macro regime signal (Sisyphus ms-009, ms-010)
BND 2-day cumulative return < 0 — bond flight signal, exits to BIL (FNGG ms-001)
TMF max-drawdown > 7% — bond dislocation crash guard (FNGG ms-006, ms-007)

volatility_index_proxies.what_they_signal: VIX futures market stress. Standard deviation of UVXY price is a real-time volatility gauge. When UVXY std-dev spikes, regime has shifted. Note: UVXY is also in generator_approved as a holdable asset — when used as lhs-val in a condition it functions as a signal.
confirmed_uses: UVXY standard-deviation-price(5) vs UVXY threshold — vol regime gate (FNGG ms-007)
UVXY standard-deviation-return(10) < 10 — low vol confirmation before entering SVXY (FNGG ms-002)

equity_proxies.what_they_signal: Leveraged price levels used as dynamic thresholds. QLD (2x QQQ) price vs QLD threshold = bull market price confirmation. PSQ (1x inverse QQQ) as RSI threshold comparator = bear pressure gauge. These are already in generator_approved (UPRO) or close analogs — their use as signal assets is about comparing leveraged price levels as regime confirmation.
confirmed_uses: QLD current-price vs QLD threshold — bull market price confirmation (Sisyphus ms-009, ms-010)
UPRO RSI(60) > 31 — bull regime RSI floor before entering leveraged positions (Sisyphus ms-009, ms-010)
PSQ as RSI threshold comparator — inverse QQQ pressure signal (FNGG ms-002, Sisyphus ms-002, ms-006)

credit_market.what_they_signal: Investment-grade credit market health. IGIB RSI dropping signals credit stress before it appears in equity prices. CORP cumulative return vs BIL measures whether credit is outperforming cash — a leading risk-on/risk-off indicator.
confirmed_uses: IGIB RSI(10) vs SPY RSI threshold — credit market regime signal for EM routing (Sisyphus ms-004, ms-007)
CORP 60-day cumulative return vs BIL — credit excess return over cash as risk-on gate (Sisyphus ms-006)

generator_rules.leveraged_bear_note: SQQQ, SOXS, SPXS, TECS require a confirmed bearish signal. Never place in the else branch (normal market conditions). Holding a bear ETF in a bull market causes severe decay losses.
generator_rules.volatility_long_note: UVXY and VIXM lose 75-90% annually from futures roll decay in calm markets. Use only in crash/spike branches with strict exit conditions.
generator_rules.volatility_short_note: SVIX and SVXY have extreme crash risk. Always require a max-drawdown guard as the outermost condition.
generator_rules.signal_asset_rule: TLT, IEF, BND, IGIB, CORP, IEI, QLD, PSQ, QID may appear as lhs-val or rhs-val in conditions ONLY. They must never appear as asset leaf nodes. Violating this rule produces invalid JSON (unrecognized ticker in asset node).
generator_rules.bond_signal_principle: The relative change between bonds (IEF/TLT), equities (SPY/QQQ), and volatility (UVXY) indicates market direction BEFORE price drawdown appears in equity ETFs. Use bond RSI drops, bond MA crossovers, and credit return vs cash as leading crash indicators. A strategy that only watches equity price will always exit too late.

confirmed_parameter_priors.description: Parameters confirmed by appearing identically in 2+ independent high-Sharpe symphonies. Use these as generator defaults — do not randomize away from these without specific reason.
confirmed_parameter_priors.source: Research Phase pilot: FNGG (Sharpe 2.13) + Sisyphus V0.0 (Sharpe 1.92)

confirmed_in: Sisyphus ms-001, ms-002, ms-004, ms-005, ms-007
priors.note: RSI(10) is the dominant short-term momentum window across both symphonies. Default to 10 unless a specific regime requires longer lookback.

TQQQ cumulative-return > 5.5% appears as the consensus bull-entry gate across two independent symphonies. This is a high-confidence prior. Window varies (1-day to 60-day) but threshold is stable at 5.5%.
SPY price compared to its own threshold (moving average crossover) is a universal regime confirmation. Always include at least one SPY price-level condition in regime_router patterns.
60-day is the dominant long-lookback window for cumulative return calculations. Use for bear-market circuit breakers (QQQ 60-day return < -12% type conditions).
UPRO RSI(60) > 31 used as minimum RSI floor before entering leveraged bull positions. Confirms trend is not deeply oversold. Needs Beta Baller confirmation to raise confidence.
max-drawdown(2) > 10% on SVXY is the standard Volmageddon-style crash guard. Short window catches rapid crashes. This is a MANDATORY pattern whenever SVXY is held.
UVXY and VIXM appear together as paired volatility hedges, not alone. UVXY for acute spikes, VIXM for sustained elevated vol. Use both when routing to volatility defensive positions.

critical_risk_notes.description: Historical events Claude must know. These facts may be underweighted without explicit injection.
critical_risk_notes.SVXY: Dropped 93% intraday on Feb 5 2018 (Volmageddon). A rebalancing cascade caused near-total wipeout in hours. Never hold without an outer max-drawdown crash guard. Confirmed guard parameters: max-drawdown(window=2) > 10%.
critical_risk_notes.UVXY: Loses 75-90% of value annually from VIX futures roll decay in low-volatility regimes. Only useful during volatility spikes. Short hold periods only — days not weeks.
critical_risk_notes.TQQQ: 2022 bear market drawdown: -80%. 3x leverage amplifies crashes non-linearly. Guard conditions are mandatory. Confirmed momentum gate: cumulative-return > 5.5% before entering.
critical_risk_notes.SOXL: 2022 drawdown: -90%. Semiconductor concentration makes it the most volatile 3x ETF in the universe. Requires strict guard conditions.
critical_risk_notes.TECL: 2022 drawdown: -75%. Similar profile to SOXL but technology-broad rather than semiconductor-specific.
critical_risk_notes.SQQQ: 3x short Nasdaq. Severe negative decay in bull markets — loses value daily from leverage rebalancing. Use only when bearish signal is actively confirmed.
critical_risk_notes.SPXS: 3x short S&P 500. Same decay risk as SQQQ. Never hold in neutral or bullish regime branches.
critical_risk_notes.SOXS: 3x short semiconductors. Extreme decay outside confirmed bear markets.
critical_risk_notes.TECS: 3x short technology. Same decay profile as SOXS.
critical_risk_notes.TMF: 2022 was historically unusual — 3x long treasury fell simultaneously with equities due to rapid Fed rate hikes. The historical negative equity correlation is unreliable post-2022. Do not assume TMF will protect in all equity drawdowns.
critical_risk_notes.TLT: Signal asset only (v1.1). Use as condition signal (RSI, MA return, cumulative return) to detect bond-market stress as a leading equity crash indicator. Do not hold as position until Research Phase confirms backtest value as safe haven.

known_high_correlation_pairs.description: Pre-loaded to prevent Generator from combining near-identical assets.

pairs.note: Near identical — both 3x S&P 500. Never use both in the same strategy.
Near identical — both 3x tech. Use filter top-1 to choose between them.
Both 3x tech-adjacent. No meaningful diversification. Use filter top-1.
Near identical FANG+ products. Out of generator universe but flag if seen in community symphonies.
Inverse pair — short vol vs long vol. Never hold simultaneously.

full_reference.description: Complete ticker list for R1 community symphony analysis. Not injected into Generator prompts — used by research_phase.py to categorize community symphony tickers.

spy_correlation_thresholds.description: Thresholds for R1 correlation classification task.

uncorrelated.label: Independent return stream — highest portfolio value

negative_correlation.label: Inverse SPY — rare, extremely valuable as hedge
